cluster_name: Typhoon Maysak kills 39 in Guangxi ahead of Super Typhoon Bavi
event_summary: Typhoon Maysak struck Guangxi, China, killing 39 after a dam burst at Liulan Reservoir displaced 130,000. Super Typhoon Bavi bore down on Taiwan and Fujian with landfall expected July 11; monsoon landslides killed at least 13 at Rohingya camps in Bangladesh.
tldr: The most striking detail in this corpus comes from HKFP’s on-the-ground body article: Guangxi residents reported receiving no warning before the Liulan Reservoir dam burst, responsible for 26 of the 39 deaths, and described flooding unprecedented in living memory. China’s state-aligned Sixth Tone sidestepped the warning-failure angle entirely, covering instead the cultural toll: jasmine fields inundated during peak tea-harvest season. Coverage across 24 outlets and 13 countries split between the immediate Maysak death count and anticipatory dread of Super Typhoon Bavi, which meteorologists described as possibly the most powerful since 2024. The Rohingya camp landslides in Cox’s Bazar — killing at least 13, including children struck while attending class — were tracked mainly by Indian and UK outlets; Southeast Asian press concentrated on the regional typhoon track. VnExpress alone reported that around 900 snakes escaped a flooded Guangxi snake farm, illustrating the Southeast Asian regional texture largely absent from Western and wire coverage.

evidence.quote: About 130,000 people have been evacuated from the Guangxi region while thousands of rescuers have been deployed.
Rescuers pull bodies from the mud after heavy monsoon rains batter the south-eastern coast.
The death toll from devastating floods in southern China’s Guangxi region rose to 39 on Thursday, with nine people still missing, state media reported, as residents went about cleaning up.
Twenty-six deaths were linked to a dam breach at Liulan Reservoir, with seven still missing there, state news agency Xinhua reported.
“We never received any warning. If we had received a warning, our losses would have been much less,” he added.

Worried residents in China are preparing for Super Typhoon Bavi to land, stocking up on supplies and rethinking travel plans.
Three days earlier, landslides killed at least eight people at Rohingya camps in the area.

silences.what_they_covered_instead: Philstar reported exclusively on domestic Signal No. 2 warnings over Batanes for Typhoon Inday (Bavi), with no coverage of the 39-death toll in Guangxi or the Bangladesh Rohingya camp landslides.

Japan Times covered only flight and ferry cancellations from Bavi's track toward remote Japanese islands; China's death toll and the Bangladesh landslides received no coverage from Japanese outlets in this cluster.

single_outlet_findings.finding: Only outlet with a full body extraction; provides on-the-ground resident testimony that no warning was issued before the Liulan Reservoir dam burst, with one resident describing it as the worst flooding 'never in history.'

Sole outlet to cover agricultural losses: Guangxi's jasmine fields inundated during peak tea-harvest season, framing the disaster through cultural-economic loss rather than the death toll or warning failures.
Only outlet to invoke El Niño as a causal driver, quoting experts who warn the return of El Niño may partly explain escalating extreme weather events in China.

Alone in reporting that around 900 snakes escaped a flooded snake farm in Hengzhou, Guangxi, with villagers joining rescuers in the search — an ecological-agricultural angle with no parallel elsewhere in the corpus.

Covers the typhoon exclusively under the Philippine domestic name 'Typhoon Inday' with Signal No. 2 alerts over Batanes, framing it as a local domestic threat distinct from all other outlets that use the international name Bavi.

Only outlet to report Indonesia's exposure: BMKG urged residents in eastern Indonesia to remain vigilant for strong winds and waves from Cyclone Bavi — a geographic impact entirely absent from all other coverage.

bottom_line: Typhoon Maysak's death toll reached 39 in Guangxi, with survivors reporting no warning before the Liulan Reservoir dam burst — a detail surfaced by HKFP's body extraction but absent from state-aligned Chinese coverage — even as Super Typhoon Bavi, potentially the strongest in years, bore down on Taiwan and Fujian. Across 24 outlets and 13 countries, the simultaneous crises in China, India, and Bangladesh's Rohingya camps were covered with sharply divergent geographic emphasis: Indian press on domestic monsoon chaos, Southeast Asian press on typhoon track, and only UK and Indian outlets giving sustained attention to the Rohingya humanitarian toll.
